A painted milepost stands beside a wagon rut, and the ditch beyond it is too quiet. Bandits rise from the grass when the party slows to read the sign.
Armed toughs have dragged a log across the road and call it a lawful toll. Their smiles fade when challenged.
A stone culvert under the road hums with thin wings. Stirges pour out when hooves or boots drum across the hollow span.
Rain has smeared the painted arrows on a fork marker. Comparing wheel ruts and old camp ash reveals which branch sees real traffic.
A small purse lies pressed into a muddy wagon rut, almost hidden under straw. The contents are damp but spendable.
A bridge board lifts underfoot with a rotten crack. A quick shift of weight keeps the crossing from becoming a plunge into the ditch below.
An overturned cart blocks one wheel track, its driver gone and its cargo scattered. Fresh footprints lead away from the road at a run.
A new detour sign points toward a narrow lane that does not appear on any old route list. The paint is fresh, and so are the bootprints around it.
A messenger waves from beside a lame horse, clutching a sealed packet. Delivering it onward could matter more than reaching camp on time.
Berries ripen in the hedgerow beside the road, dusty but sweet. The best clusters hang just beyond thorny branches.
Travelers have trampled most of the verge, but hardy herbs still grow around a milestone. Cutting them cleanly avoids grit and road filth.
A torn sack has spilled grain under the wagon tracks. Quick sorting saves enough clean kernels before birds and mud take the rest.
A rain-filled ditch beside the road wriggles with trapped minnows. They are easy to net before the puddle drains into the culvert.
A deep pool below a bridge holds trout in the shadow of the span. Dropping a line from above spooks them unless handled gently.
Crayfish hide among stones at a shallow ford where wagons slow. The catch is good if the water is not churned first.
Loose chips of pale stone lie where an old milestone was carved nearby. A little searching finds larger usable pieces in the roadside bank.
A roadcut exposes red clay beneath roots and gravel. It can be gathered quickly before traffic shakes more dirt into it.
Flint nodules turn up where wagon wheels bite through thin soil. Sorting sharp useful pieces from broken gravel takes care.
A roadside tree has fallen across the verge after a storm. Its trunk is clear enough to cut before traffic needs the space.
A wrecked wagon has been pushed into the ditch, its wheels gone but its boards still useful. Pulling nails takes longer than cutting wood.
A cracked signpost leans where a newer marker has replaced it. The dry post can be salvaged without confusing travelers.
A milestone has been smeared with mud to hide fresh arrows buried around it. Goblins wait for travelers to stop and clean the marker.
A gang in red cloaks blocks the lane with a broken handcart and practiced smiles. Their hands stay near cudgels as they count purses.
An abandoned wagon creaks forward though no mule pulls it. Rusted tools leap from the bed as the old ward inside misfires.
Tiny marks beneath the milestone number form a Lord's Alliance route code. Reading it reveals which side track avoids a blocked bridge.
A coachwheel snaps ahead and sends cargo tumbling across the road. Fast reflexes keep the pileup from rolling into the party.
A tollkeeper's old purse is nailed under a bridge plank, wrapped against rain. Its owner either forgot it or never survived to return.
A supply wagon sits in the road with dead horses and no driver. Tracks lead into the brush, but one crate still knocks from inside.
A driver's boot buckle hangs from a thorn branch beside drag marks. Following the trail could find a prisoner before the captors change camps.
A toll crew presents a writ with a real seal and a false date. Proving the forgery means finding the clerk who sold the seal.
A famous inn's sign lies split in a roadside ditch miles from its building. Fresh wheel ruts lead toward a woodlot full of voices.
A rain-soaked map points from three milestones to an old hideout under the road. Someone circled the entrance and wrote 'tonight'.
Apples from an old roadside tree litter the grass after wind. The best fruit lies under nettles and boot tracks.
Garlic-scented greens grow along a neglected hedge. Cutting clean bunches keeps dirt out of the harvest.
A bundle of drying herbs hangs from a way shrine, left as an offering for those in need. Taking only a share honors the custom.
Trout hold in the shade beneath a road bridge. A hook must drift naturally under the arch before wagon noise spooks them.
Minnows gather in a stone culvert after rain. Scooping them out is easy once the grate is cleared.
Frogs crowd a wayside pond where carts stop to water mules. They leap for cover whenever hooves approach.
A pile of cut stones for milestone repair lies under canvas. Some blocks are sound enough to salvage with permission or discretion.
A washed-out lane exposes a vein of hard gravel ideal for repairs. Filling sacks takes work before carts churn it to mud.
Bronze fittings from a broken wagon lie scattered in a ditch. Sorting useful pieces from junk takes a careful eye.
A signpost snapped in the night and left dry oak beneath the paint. It can be cut down once the directions are copied.
A ruined cart has two straight side planks still intact. Pulling the pegs instead of hacking them free preserves the wood.
A windfall tree blocks half the lane and draws complaints from travelers. Cutting it clears the road and yields useful timber.
